---
title: "nitrix.register.diffeomorphic"
generated: true
signature-hash: 52552ac320310c8b
description: "Diffeomorphic registration recipe: log-domain Demons."
listing:
  id: nitrix-register-diffeomorphic-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-register-diffeomorphic/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.register.diffeomorphic`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Diffeomorphic registration recipe: log-domain Demons.

The representative diffeomorphic algorithm of Vercauteren, Pennec, Perchant
and Ayache (2009), parametrised by a *stationary velocity field* $v$
whose exponential $\varphi = \exp(v)$ (scaling-and-squaring, via
[`integrate_velocity_field`](nitrix-geometry-grid/integrate_velocity_field.qmd)) is a diffeomorphism by
construction.  Each iteration is an operator splitting:

1. **ESM demons force** -- a closed-form per-voxel velocity update
   $u = (F - M \circ \varphi)\, J / (|J|^2 + \alpha^2 (F - M \circ \varphi)^2)$
   with the symmetric gradient
   $J = \tfrac{1}{2}(\nabla F + \nabla (M \circ \varphi))$
   (efficient symmetric forces).  There is no inner solve -- this is what
   makes the diffeomorphic recipe as GPU-clean as it is.
2. **Fluid regularisation** -- Gaussian-smooth the update $u$.
3. **Log update** -- $v \leftarrow v + u$ (additive; the
   Baker-Campbell-Hausdorff term $+ \tfrac{1}{2}[v, u]$ is optional).
4. **Diffusion regularisation** -- Gaussian-smooth the velocity $v$.

The recipe proceeds coarse-to-fine over a Gaussian pyramid.  The Gaussian
smoothings are the Green's functions of the fluid/diffusion regularisers;
$\alpha$ normalises the force.  The recipe is a pure composition of the
substrate (SVF exponential, warp, gradient, Gaussian, velocity algebra) -- the
only metric-specific piece is the closed-form force.  An LNCC-driven
(SyN-style) force and a symmetric forward-plus-inverse variant are the
documented upgrade paths.

The above is the *algebra* (log-domain SVF) representation -- the exact oracle.
The default `representation='group'` ([`DemonsSpec`](nitrix-register-diffeomorphic/DemonsSpec.qmd)) instead carries the
*displacement* and uses the greedy compositive update (warp directly, compose
the regularised increment -- no per-iteration exponential, roughly two gathers
per iteration versus seven), recovering the velocity once at finalisation via
[`field_log`](nitrix-geometry-deformation/field_log.qmd).  The greedy update is not the SVF fixed
point, so the two representations agree on synthetic recovery to tolerance, not
field-wise.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:__module__ -->
### References

Vercauteren, T., Pennec, X., Perchant, A., & Ayache, N. (2009). Diffeomorphic
demons: efficient non-parametric image registration. NeuroImage, 45(1),
S61-S72. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuroimage.2008.10.040
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-register-diffeomorphic-symbols}
:::
